member.name: T:System.IdHelpers
member.summary: Provides helper methods for monotonic ID generation.

member.name: M:System.IdHelpers.CreateMonotonicId(System.DateTimeOffset)
member.summary: Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
member.param: The timestamp to embed in the identifier.
member.returns: A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.

remarks.para: This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values: - High 64 bits: from the timestamp - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter

The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically, which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike Guid.CreateVersion7, which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.

remarks.para: This method creates a function that converts MCP message requests into chat client calls, enabling an MCP client to generate text or other content using an actual AI model via the provided chat client.
The handler can process text messages, image messages, resource messages, and tool use/results as defined in the Model Context Protocol.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.AssemblyNameHelper.DefaultAssemblyName
member.summary: Cached naming information used for MCP session name/version when none is specified.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.AuthorizationRedirectDelegate
member.summary: Represents a method that handles the OAuth authorization URL and returns the authorization code.
member.param: The authorization URL that the user needs to visit.
The redirect URI where the authorization code will be sent.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the authorization code if successful, or null if the operation failed or was cancelled.

remarks.para: This delegate provides SDK consumers with full control over how the OAuth authorization flow is handled. Implementers can choose to:

item.description: Start a local HTTP server and open a browser (default behavior)
Display the authorization URL to the user for manual handling
Integrate with a custom UI or authentication flow
Use a different redirect mechanism altogether

remarks.para: The implementation should handle user interaction to visit the authorization URL and extract the authorization code from the callback. The authorization code is typically provided as a query parameter in the redirect URI callback.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions
member.summary: Provides configuration options for the .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.RedirectUri
member.summary: Gets or sets the OAuth redirect URI.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.ClientId
member.summary: Gets or sets the OAuth client ID. If not provided, the client will attempt to register dynamically.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.ClientSecret
member.summary: Gets or sets the OAuth client secret.
member.remarks: This secret is optional for public clients or when using PKCE without client authentication.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.ClientMetadataDocumentUri
member.summary: Gets or sets the HTTPS URL pointing to this client's metadata document.
member.remarks: When specified, and when the authorization server metadata reports client_id_metadata_document_supported = true, the OAuth client will respond to challenges by sending this URL as the client identifier instead of performing dynamic client registration.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.Scopes
member.summary: Gets or sets the OAuth scopes to request.

remarks.para: When specified, these scopes will be used instead of the scopes advertised by the protected resource. If not specified, the provider will use the scopes from the protected resource metadata.
Common OAuth scopes include "openid", "profile", and "email".

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.AuthorizationRedirectDelegate
member.summary: Gets or sets the authorization redirect delegate for handling the OAuth authorization flow.

remarks.para: This delegate is responsible for handling the OAuth authorization URL and obtaining the authorization code. If not specified, a default implementation will be used that prompts the user to enter the code manually.
Custom implementations might open a browser, start an HTTP listener, or use other mechanisms to capture the authorization code from the OAuth redirect.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.AuthServerSelector
member.summary: Gets or sets the authorization server selector function.

remarks.para: This function is used to select which authorization server to use when multiple servers are available. If not specified, the first available server will be selected.
The function receives a list of available authorization server URIs and should return the selected server, or null if no suitable server is found.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.DynamicClientRegistration
member.summary: Gets or sets the options to use during dynamic client registration.
member.remarks: This value is only used when no is specified.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.AdditionalAuthorizationParameters
member.summary: Gets or sets additional parameters to include in the query string of the OAuth authorization request providing extra information or fulfilling specific requirements of the OAuth provider.

remarks.para: Parameters specified cannot override or append to any automatically set parameters like the "redirect_uri", which should instead be configured via .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ClientOAuthOptions.TokenCache
member.summary: Gets or sets the token cache to use for storing and retrieving tokens beyond the lifetime of the transport. If none is provided, tokens will be cached with the transport.

member.summary: A generic implementation of an OAuth authorization provider.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class using the specified options.
member.param: The MCP server URL.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata
member.summary: Represents the resource metadata for OAuth authorization as defined in RFC 9728.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.Resource
member.summary: Gets or sets the resource URI.
member.value: The protected resource's resource identifier.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL. When omitted, the MCP authentication handler infers the resource URI from the incoming request only when serving the default /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource endpoint. If a custom ResourceMetadataUri is configured, Resource must be explicitly set. Automatic inference only works with the default endpoint pattern.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.AuthorizationServers
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of authorization server URIs.
member.value: A JSON array containing a list of OAuth authorization server issuer identifiers for authorization servers that can be used with this protected resource.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.BearerMethodsSupported
member.summary: Gets or sets the supported bearer token methods.
member.value: A JSON array containing a list of the supported methods of sending an OAuth 2.0 bearer token to the protected resource. Defined values are ["header", "body", "query"].
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.ScopesSupported
member.summary: Gets or sets the supported scopes.
member.value: A JSON array containing a list of scope values that are used in authorization requests to request access to this protected resource.
member.remarks: RECOMMENDED.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.JwksUri
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL of the protected resource's JSON Web Key (JWK) Set document.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL. This document contains public keys belonging to the protected resource, such as signing keys that the resource server uses to sign resource responses. This URL MUST use the HTTPS scheme.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.ResourceSigningAlgValuesSupported
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of the JWS signing algorithms supported by the protected resource for signing resource responses.
member.value: A JSON array containing a list of the JWS signing algorithms (alg values) supported by the protected resource for signing resource responses.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL. No default algorithms are implied if this entry is omitted. The value "none" MUST NOT be used.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.ResourceName
member.summary: Gets or sets the human-readable name of the protected resource intended for display to the end user.
member.remarks: RECOMMENDED. It is recommended that protected resource metadata include this field. The value of this field MAY be internationalized.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.ResourceDocumentation
member.summary: Gets or sets the URI to the resource documentation.
member.value: The URL of a page containing human-readable information that developers might want or need to know when using the protected resource.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.ResourcePolicyUri
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL of a page containing human-readable information about the protected resource's requirements.
member.value: The URL of a page that contains information about how the client can use the data provided by the protected resource.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.ResourceTosUri
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL of a page containing human-readable information about the protected resource's terms of service.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL. The value of this field MAY be internationalized.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.TlsClientCertificateBoundAccessTokens
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether there is protected resource support for mutual-TLS client certificate-bound access tokens.
member.value: if there's protected resource support for mutual-TLS client certificate-bound access tokens; otherwise, . The default is .
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.AuthorizationDetailsTypesSupported
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of the authorization details type values supported by the resource server.
member.value: A JSON array containing a list of the authorization details type values supported by the resource server when the authorization_details request parameter is used.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.DpopSigningAlgValuesSupported
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of the JWS algorithm values supported by the resource server for validating DPoP proof JWTs.
member.value: A JSON array containing a list of the JWS alg values supported by the resource server for validating Demonstrating Proof of Possession (DPoP) proof JWTs.
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.DpopBoundAccessTokensRequired
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the protected resource always requires the use of DPoP-bound access tokens.
member.value: if the protected resource always requires the use of DPoP-bound access tokens; otherwise, . The default is .
member.remarks: OPTIONAL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Authentication.ProtectedResourceMetadata.WwwAuthenticateScope
member.summary:
Used internally by the client to get or set the scope specified as a WWW-Authenticate header parameter. This should be preferred over using the ScopesSupported property.
The scopes included in the WWW-Authenticate challenge MAY match scopes_supported, be a subset or superset of it, or an alternative collection that is neither a strict subset nor superset. Clients MUST NOT assume any particular set relationship between the challenged scope set and scopes_supported. Clients MUST treat the scopes provided in the challenge as authoritative for satisfying the current request.
https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/authorization#protected-resource-metadata-discovery-requirements

member.remarks: This transport connects to an MCP server over HTTP using SSE or Streamable HTTP, allowing for real-time server-to-client communication with standard HTTP requests. Unlike the , this transport connects to an existing server rather than launching a new process.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions
member.summary: Provides options for configuring instances.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.Endpoint
member.summary: Gets or sets the base address of the server for SSE connections.
member.exception: The value is .
The value is not an absolute URI, or does not use the HTTP or HTTPS scheme.

members.member:
Gets or sets the transport mode to use for the connection.
The transport mode to use for the connection. The default is .
When set to (the default), the client will first attempt to use Streamable HTTP transport and automatically fall back to SSE transport if the server doesn't support it.
Streamable HTTP transport specification. HTTP with SSE transport specification.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets a transport identifier used for logging purposes.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.ConnectionTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets a timeout used to establish the initial connection to the SSE server.
member.value: The timeout used to establish the initial connection to the SSE server. The default is 30 seconds.
member.remarks:
This timeout controls how long the client waits for:
The initial HTTP connection to be established with the SSE server.
The endpoint event to be received, which indicates the message endpoint URL.
If the timeout expires before the connection is established, a is thrown.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.AdditionalHeaders
member.summary: Gets or sets custom HTTP headers to include in requests to the SSE server.
member.remarks: Use this property to specify custom HTTP headers that should be sent with each request to the server.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.KnownSessionId
member.summary: Gets or sets a session identifier that should be reused when connecting to a Streamable HTTP server.

remarks.para: When non-, the transport assumes the server already created the session and will include the specified session identifier in every HTTP request. This allows reconnecting to an existing session created in a previous process. This option is only supported by the Streamable HTTP transport mode.

Clients should pair this with
to skip the initialization handshake when rehydrating a previously negotiated session.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.OwnsSession
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether this transport endpoint is responsible for ending the session on dispose.
member.remarks: When (default), the transport sends a DELETE request that informs the server the session is complete. Set this to when creating a transport used solely to bootstrap session information that will later be resumed elsewhere.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.OAuth
member.summary: Gets or sets the authorization provider to use for authentication.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.MaxReconnectionAttempts
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of consecutive reconnection attempts when an SSE stream is disconnected.
member.value: The maximum number of reconnection attempts. The default is 5.
member.remarks: When an SSE stream is disconnected (e.g., due to a network issue), the client will attempt to reconnect using the Last-Event-ID header to resume from where it left off. This property controls how many consecutive reconnection attempts are made before giving up. The counter resets to zero on each successful stream read, so this value only limits consecutive failures.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.DefaultReconnectionInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the default interval at which the client attempts reconnection after an SSE stream is disconnected.

remarks.para: The default value is 1 second.
If the server sends a message specifying a different reconnection interval, that new value will be used for all subsequent reconnection attempts for that stream.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.AutoDetect
member.summary: Automatically detect the appropriate transport by trying Streamable HTTP first, then falling back to SSE if that fails. This is the recommended mode for maximum compatibility.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.StreamableHttp
member.summary: Use only the Streamable HTTP transport.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.Sse
member.summary: Use only the HTTP with SSE transport.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport
member.summary: Represents a transport mechanism for Model Context Protocol (MCP) client-to-server communication.

remarks.para: The interface abstracts the communication layer between MCP clients and servers, allowing different transport protocols to be used interchangeably.
When creating an , is typically used, and is provided with the based on expected server configuration.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport.Name
member.summary: Gets a transport identifier, used for logging purposes.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport.ConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously establishes a transport session with an MCP server and returns a transport for the duplex message stream.
member.param: The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: An interface for the duplex message stream.

remarks.para: This method is responsible for initializing the connection to the server using the specific transport mechanism implemented by the derived class. The returned interface provides methods to send and receive messages over the established connection.
The lifetime of the returned instance is typically managed by the that uses this transport. When the client is disposed, it will dispose the transport session as well.
This method is used by to initialize the connection.

member.exception: The transport connection could not be established.

remarks.para: This property provides identification details about the connected server, including its name and version. It is populated during the initialization handshake and is available after a successful connection.
This information can be useful for logging, debugging, compatibility checks, and displaying server information to users.

member.exception: The client is not connected.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ServerInstructions
member.summary: Gets any instructions describing how to use the connected server and its features.

remarks.para: This property contains instructions provided by the server during initialization that explain how to effectively use its capabilities. They should focus on guidance that helps a model use the server effectively and should avoid duplicating tool, prompt, or resource descriptions.
This can be used by clients to improve an LLM's understanding of how to use the server. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model and can be added to a system prompt.

member.summary: Invokes a tool on the server as a task for long-running operations.
member.param: The name of the tool to call on the server.
An optional dictionary of arguments to pass to the tool.
Metadata for task augmentation, including optional TTL. If , an empty metadata is used.
An optional progress reporter for server notifications.
Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: An representing the created task. Use to poll for status updates and to retrieve the final result.
member.exception: is .
The request failed or the server returned an error response.

remarks.para: Task-augmented tool calls allow long-running operations to be executed asynchronously. Instead of blocking until the tool completes, the server immediately returns a task identifier that can be used to poll for status updates and retrieve the final result.
The server must advertise task support via capabilities.tasks.requests.tools.call and the tool must have execution.taskSupport set to "optional" or "required".

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers
member.summary: Provides a container for handlers used in the creation of an MCP client.

remarks.para: This class provides a centralized collection of delegates that implement various capabilities of the Model Context Protocol.

Each handler in this class corresponds to a specific client endpoint in the Model Context Protocol and is responsible for processing a particular type of message. The handlers are used to customize the behavior of the MCP client by providing implementations for the various protocol operations.

When a server sends a message to the client, the appropriate handler is invoked to process it according to the protocol specification. Which handler is selected is done based on an ordinal, case-sensitive string comparison.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.NotificationHandlers
member.summary: Gets or sets notification handlers to register with the client.

remarks.para: When constructed, the client will enumerate these handlers, which might contain multiple handlers per notification method key, once. The client will not re-enumerate the sequence after initialization.

Notification handlers allow the client to respond to server-sent notifications for specific methods. Each key in the collection is a notification method name, and each value is a callback that will be invoked when a notification with that method is received.

Handlers provided via will be registered with the client for the lifetime of the client. For transient handlers, you can use to register a handler that can then be unregistered by disposing of the returned from the method.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.RootsHandler
member.summary: Gets or sets the handler for requests.
member.remarks: This handler is invoked when the server sends a request to retrieve available roots. The handler receives request parameters and should return a containing the collection of available roots.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.ElicitationHandler
member.summary: Gets or sets the handler for processing requests.

remarks.para: This handler function is called when an MCP server requests the client to provide additional information during interactions. The client must set this property for the elicitation capability to work.
The handler receives message parameters and a cancellation token. It should return a containing the response to the elicitation request.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.SamplingHandler
member.summary: Gets or sets the handler for processing requests.

remarks.para: This handler function is called when an MCP server requests the client to generate content using an AI model. The client must set this property for the sampling capability to work.
The handler receives message parameters, a progress reporter for updates, and a cancellation token. It should return a containing the generated content.
You can create a handler using the extension method with any implementation of .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.TaskStatusHandler
member.summary: Gets or sets the handler for processing notifications.

remarks.para: This handler is called when the server sends a task status notification to inform the client about changes to a task's state. These notifications are optional and clients MUST NOT rely on receiving them.
The handler receives the updated object containing the current task state, including its status, status message, and timestamps.
This handler is typically used to update UI or trigger actions based on task progress without requiring explicit polling.

member.summary: This API supports the logging infrastructure and is not intended to be used directly from your code. It is subject to change in the future.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions
member.summary: Provides configuration options for creating instances.
member.remarks: These options are typically passed to when creating a client. They define client capabilities, protocol version, and other client-specific settings.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.ClientInfo
member.summary: Gets or sets information about this client implementation, including its name and version.

remarks.para: This information is sent to the server during initialization to identify the client. It's often displayed in server logs and can be used for debugging and compatibility checks.
When not specified, information sourced from the current process is used.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.Capabilities
member.summary: Gets or sets the client capabilities to advertise to the server.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.ProtocolVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the protocol version to request from the server, using a date-based versioning scheme.

remarks.para: The protocol version is a key part of the initialization handshake. The client and server must agree on a compatible protocol version to communicate successfully.

If non-, this version will be sent to the server, and the handshake will fail if the version in the server's response does not match this version. If , the client will request the latest version supported by the server but will allow any supported version that the server advertises in its response.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.InitializationTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets a timeout for the client-server initialization handshake sequence.
member.value: The timeout for the client-server initialization handshake sequence. The default value is 60 seconds.

remarks.para: This timeout determines how long the client will wait for the server to respond during the initialization protocol handshake. If the server doesn't respond within this timeframe, an exception is thrown.
Setting an appropriate timeout prevents the client from hanging indefinitely when connecting to unresponsive servers.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.Handlers
member.summary: Gets or sets the container of handlers used by the client for processing protocol messages.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.TaskStore
member.summary: Gets or sets the task store for managing client-side tasks.

remarks.para: When a task store is configured, the client will support task-augmented requests from the server. This allows the server to request sampling or elicitation as tasks, which the client executes asynchronously and allows the server to poll for status and results.
If not set, task-augmented requests will not be supported, and the client will not advertise task capabilities to the server.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.SendTaskStatusNotifications
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the client should send task status notifications to the server.
member.value: to send task status notifications; otherwise. The default is .
member.remarks: When enabled and a is configured, the client will send optional notifications/tasks/status notifications to inform the server of task state changes. Servers MUST NOT rely on receiving these notifications and should continue polling via tasks/get.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientPrompt
member.summary: Represents a named prompt that can be retrieved from an MCP server and invoked with arguments.

remarks.para: This class provides a client-side wrapper around a prompt defined on an MCP server. It allows retrieving the prompt's content by sending a request to the server with optional arguments. Instances of this class are typically obtained by calling .
Each prompt has a name and optionally a description, and it can be invoked with arguments to produce customized prompt content from the server.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientPrompt.#ctor(ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The instance to use for invoking the prompt.
The protocol definition describing the prompt's metadata.

remarks.para: This constructor enables reusing cached prompt definitions across different instances without needing to call on every reconnect. This is particularly useful in scenarios where prompt definitions are stable and network round-trips should be minimized.
The provided must represent a prompt that is actually available on the server associated with the . Attempting to invoke a prompt that doesn't exist on the server will result in an .

member.exception: or is .

For most common use cases, you can use the more convenient and properties instead of accessing the directly.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool
member.summary: Provides an that calls a tool via an .

remarks.para: The class encapsulates an along with a description of a tool available via that client, allowing it to be invoked as an . This enables integration with AI models that support function calling capabilities.
Tools retrieved from an MCP server can be customized for model presentation using methods like and without changing the underlying tool functionality.
Typically, you would get instances of this class by calling the method on an instance.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.#ctor(ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)

The protocol definition describing the tool's metadata and schema.
The JSON serialization options governing argument serialization. If , is used.

remarks.para: This constructor enables reusing cached tool definitions across different instances without needing to call on every reconnect. This is particularly useful in scenarios where tool definitions are stable and network round-trips should be minimized.
The provided must represent a tool that is actually available on the server associated with the . Attempting to invoke a tool that doesn't exist on the server will result in an .

member.exception: or is .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.ProtocolTool
member.summary: Gets the protocol type for this instance.
member.remarks: This property provides direct access to the underlying protocol representation of the tool, which can be useful for advanced scenarios or when implementing custom MCP client extensions. It contains the original metadata about the tool as provided by the server, including its name, description, and schema information before any customizations applied through methods like or .

member.summary: Gets the tool's title.

member.summary: Invokes the tool on the server.
member.param: An optional dictionary of arguments to pass to the tool. Each key represents a parameter name, and its associated value represents the argument value.

An optional to have progress notifications reported to it. Setting this to a non- value will result in a progress token being included in the call, and any resulting progress notifications during the operation routed to this instance.
Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: A task containing the from the tool execution. The response includes the tool's output content, which can be structured data, text, or an error message.
member.remarks: The base method is overridden to invoke this method. The only difference in behavior is that serializes the resulting such that the returned is a containing the serialized . This method is intended to be called directly by user code, whereas the base is intended to be used polymorphically via the base class, typically as part of an operation.
member.exception: The server could not find the requested tool, or the server encountered an error while processing the request.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithName(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to return the specified name from its property.
member.param: The model-facing name to give the tool.
member.returns: A new instance of with the provided name.

remarks.para: This method is useful for optimizing the tool name for specific models or for prefixing the tool name with a namespace to avoid conflicts.
Changing the name can help with:

list.item: Making the tool name more intuitive for the model.
Preventing name collisions when using tools from multiple sources.
Creating specialized versions of a general tool for specific contexts.

remarks.para: When invoking , the MCP server will still be called with the original tool name, so no mapping is required on the server side. This new name only affects the value returned from this instance's .

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithDescription(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to return the specified description from its property.
member.param: The description to give the tool.

remarks.para: Changing the description can help the model better understand the tool's purpose or provide more context about how the tool should be used. This is particularly useful when:

list.item: The original description is too technical or lacks clarity for the model.
You want to add example usage scenarios to improve the model's understanding.
You need to tailor the tool's description for specific model requirements.

remarks.para: When invoking , the MCP server will still be called with the original tool description, so no mapping is required on the server side. This new description only affects the value returned from this instance's .

member.returns: A new instance of with the provided description.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithProgress(System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue})
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to report progress via the specified .
member.param: The to which progress notifications should be reported.

remarks.para: Adding an to the tool does not impact how it is reported to any AI model. Rather, when the tool is invoked, the request to the MCP server will include a unique progress token, and any progress notifications issued by the server with that progress token while the operation is in flight will be reported to the instance.
Only one can be specified at a time. Calling again overwrites any previously specified progress instance.

member.returns: A new instance of , configured with the provided progress instance.
member.exception: is .

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithMeta(System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to include the specified metadata in tool call requests.
member.param: The metadata to include in tool call requests. This will be serialized as the _meta field in the JSON-RPC request parameters.

remarks.para: Adding metadata to the tool allows you to pass additional protocol-level information with each tool call. This can be useful for tracing, logging, or passing context information to the server.
Only one metadata object can be specified at a time. Calling again will overwrite any previously specified metadata object. If passed , any previously supplied metadata will be removed.

The metadata is passed through to the server as-is, merged with any protocol-level metadata such as progress tokens when is also used. If a is passed to , the metadata from both and its will be merged, preferring values from the in case of conflicts.

member.returns: A new instance of , configured with the provided metadata.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientTransport
member.summary: Provides a implemented via "stdio" (standard input/output).

remarks.para: This transport launches an external process and communicates with it through standard input and output streams. It's used to connect to MCP servers launched and hosted in child processes.

The transport manages the entire lifecycle of the process: starting it with specified command-line arguments and environment variables, handling output, and properly terminating the process when the transport is closed.

member.summary: Gets or sets the command to execute to start the server process.

member.summary: Gets or sets the arguments to pass to the server process when it is started.

member.summary: Gets or sets a transport identifier used for logging purposes.

member.summary: Gets or sets the working directory for the server process.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientTransportOptions.EnvironmentVariables
member.summary: Gets or sets environment variables to set for the server process.

remarks.para: This property allows you to specify environment variables that will be set in the server process's environment. Setting these variables is useful for passing configuration, authentication information, or runtime flags to the server without modifying its code.

By default, when starting the server process, the server process will inherit the current environment's variables, as discovered via . After those variables are found, the entries in this dictionary are used to augment and overwrite the entries read from the environment. That includes removing the variables for any of this collection's entries with a null value.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientTransportOptions.ShutdownTimeout
member.summary: Gets or sets the timeout to wait for the server to shut down gracefully.
member.value: The amount of time to wait for the server to shut down gracefully. The default is 5 seconds.

remarks.para: This property dictates how long the client should wait for the server process to exit cleanly during shutdown before forcibly terminating it. This balances giving the server enough time to clean up resources and not hanging indefinitely if a server process becomes unresponsive.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientTransportOptions.StandardErrorLines
member.summary: Gets or sets a callback that is invoked for each line of stderr received from the server process.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations
member.summary: Represents annotations that can be attached to content, resources, and resource templates.
member.remarks: Annotations enable filtering and prioritization of content for different audiences. See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations.Audience
member.summary: Gets or sets the intended audience for this content as an array of values.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations.Priority
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating how important this data is for operating the server.
member.remarks: The value is a floating-point number between 0 and 1, where 0 represents the lowest priority and 1 represents the highest priority.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations.LastModified
member.summary: Gets or sets the moment the resource was last modified.
member.remarks: The corresponding JSON should be an ISO 8601 formatted string (for example, \"2025-01-12T15:00:58Z\"). Examples of when the resource was last modified include last activity in an open file or when the resource was attached.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Argument

member.remarks: This class is used when requesting completion suggestions for a particular field or parameter. See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Argument.Value
member.summary: Gets or sets the current partial text value for which completion suggestions are requested.
member.remarks: This property represents the text that has been entered so far and for which completion options should be generated.

remarks.para: is used when binary data needs to be exchanged through the Model Context Protocol. The binary data is represented as base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes in the property.
This class inherits from , which also has a sibling implementation for text-based resources. When working with resources, the appropriate type is chosen based on the nature of the content.
See the schema for more details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a client to invoke a tool provided by the server.
member.remarks: The server will respond with a containing the result of the tool invocation. See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the tool to invoke.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments
member.summary: Gets or sets optional arguments to pass to the tool when invoking it on the server.
member.remarks: This dictionary contains the parameter values to be passed to the tool. Each key-value pair represents a parameter name and its corresponding argument value.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Task
member.summary: Gets or sets optional task metadata to augment this request with task execution.
member.remarks: When present, indicates that the requestor wants this operation executed as a task. The receiver must support task augmentation for this specific request type.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a request from a client to invoke a tool provided by the server.

remarks.para: Tool execution errors (including input validation errors, API failures, and business logic errors) should be reported inside the result object with set to , rather than as a . This allows language models to see error details and potentially self-correct in subsequent requests.

To return a validation or business-logic error from a tool method, either throw an (whose will be included in the error result), or declare the tool's return type as so it can be returned directly with set to and details in . Using as the return type gives the tool full control over both success and error responses.
Protocol-level errors (such as unknown tool names, malformed requests that fail schema validation, or server errors) should be reported as MCP protocol error responses using .
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content
member.summary: Gets or sets the response content from the tool call.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.StructuredContent
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional JSON object representing the structured result of the tool call.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool call was unsuccessful.
member.value: to signify that the tool execution failed; if it was successful.

remarks.para: Tool execution errors (including input validation errors, API failures, and business logic errors) are reported with this property set to and details in the property, rather than as protocol-level errors.

This design allows language models to receive detailed error feedback and potentially self-correct in subsequent requests. For example, if a date parameter is in the wrong format or out of range, the error message in can explain the issue, enabling the model to retry with corrected parameters.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Task
member.summary: Gets or sets the task data for the newly created task.
member.remarks: This property is populated only for task-augmented tool calls. When present, the other properties (, , ) may not be populated. The actual tool result can be retrieved later via tasks/result.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams
member.summary: Represents a notification indicating that a request has been cancelled by the client, and that any associated processing should cease immediately.
member.remarks: This class is typically used in conjunction with the method identifier. When a client sends this notification, the server should attempt to cancel any ongoing operations associated with the specified request ID.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams.RequestId
member.summary: Gets or sets the ID of the request to cancel.
member.remarks: This value must match the ID of an in-flight request that the sender wishes to cancel.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams.Reason
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional string describing the reason for the cancellation request.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters for a tasks/cancel request to explicitly cancel a task.

remarks.para: Upon receiving a valid cancellation request, receivers should attempt to stop the task execution and must transition the task to status before sending the response.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskRequestParams.TaskId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier of the task to cancel.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a tasks/cancel request.
member.remarks: The result contains the updated task state after cancellation. The task will be in status if the cancellation was successful.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.TaskId
member.summary: Gets or sets the task ID.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.Status
member.summary: Gets or sets the current status of the task (should be ).

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.StatusMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional message describing the cancellation.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.CreatedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.LastUpdatedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task status was last updated.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.TimeToLive
member.summary: Gets or sets the time to live (retention duration) from creation before the task may be deleted.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.PollInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the suggested time between status checks.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities
member.summary: Represents the capabilities that a client supports.

remarks.para: Capabilities define the features and functionality that a client can handle when communicating with an MCP server. These are advertised to the server during the initialize handshake.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Experimental
member.summary: Gets or sets experimental, non-standard capabilities that the client supports.

remarks.para: The dictionary allows clients to advertise support for features that are not yet standardized in the Model Context Protocol specification. This extension mechanism enables future protocol enhancements while maintaining backward compatibility.

Values in this dictionary are implementation-specific and should be coordinated between client and server implementations. Servers should not assume the presence of any experimental capability without checking for it first.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Roots
member.summary: Gets or sets the client's roots capability, which are entry points for resource navigation.

remarks.para: When is non-, the client indicates that it can respond to server requests for listing root URIs. Root URIs serve as entry points for resource navigation in the protocol.
The server can use to request the list of available roots from the client, which will trigger the client's .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Sampling
member.summary: Gets or sets the client's sampling capability, which indicates whether the client supports issuing requests to an LLM on behalf of the server.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Elicitation
member.summary: Gets or sets the client's elicitation capability, which indicates whether the client supports elicitation of additional information from the user on behalf of the server.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Tasks
member.summary: Gets or sets the client's tasks capability for supporting task-augmented requests.

remarks.para: The tasks capability enables servers to augment their requests with tasks for long-running operations. When present, servers can request that certain operations (like sampling or elicitation) execute asynchronously, with the ability to poll for status and retrieve results later.
See for details on configuring which operations support tasks.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Extensions
member.summary: Gets or sets optional MCP extensions that the client supports.

remarks.para: Keys are extension identifiers in reverse domain notation with an extension name (e.g., "io.modelcontextprotocol/oauth-client-credentials"), and values are per-extension settings objects. An empty object indicates support with no additional settings.
Extensions provide a framework for extending the Model Context Protocol while maintaining interoperability. Clients advertise extension support via this field during the initialization handshake.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteContext
member.summary: Represents additional context information for completion requests.
member.remarks: This context provides information that helps the server generate more relevant completion suggestions, such as previously resolved variables in a template.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteContext.Arguments
member.summary: Gets or sets previously-resolved variables in a URI template or prompt.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a client to ask a server for auto-completion suggestions.

remarks.para: is used in the Model Context Protocol completion workflow to provide intelligent suggestions for partial inputs related to resources, prompts, or other referenceable entities. The completion mechanism in MCP allows clients to request suggestions based on partial inputs. The server will respond with a containing matching values.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams.Ref
member.summary: Gets or sets the reference's information.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams.Argument
member.summary: Gets or sets the argument information for the completion request, specifying what is being completed and the current partial input.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams.Context
member.summary: Gets or sets additional, optional context for completions.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult
member.summary: Represents the server's response to a request, containing suggested values for a given argument.

remarks.para: is returned by the server in response to a request from the client. It provides suggested completions or valid values for a specific argument in a tool or resource reference.
The result contains a object with suggested values, pagination information, and the total number of available completions. This is similar to auto-completion functionality in code editors.
Clients typically use this to implement auto-suggestion features when users are inputting parameters for tool calls or resource references.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult.Completion
member.summary: Gets or sets the completion object containing the suggested values and pagination information.
member.remarks: If no completions are available for the given input, the collection will be empty.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion
member.summary: Represents a completion object in the server's response to a request.
member.remarks: See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion.Values
member.summary: Gets or sets an array of completion values (auto-suggestions) for the requested input.
member.remarks: This collection contains the actual text strings to be presented to users as completion suggestions. The array will be empty if no suggestions are available for the current input. Per the specification, this collection should not exceed 100 items.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion.Total
member.summary: Gets or sets the total number of completion options available.
member.remarks: This value can exceed the number of values actually sent in the response.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion.HasMore
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether there are additional completion options beyond those provided in the current response, even if the exact total is unknown.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompletionsCapability
member.summary: Represents the completions capability for providing auto-completion suggestions for prompt arguments and resource references.

remarks.para: When enabled, this capability allows a Model Context Protocol server to provide auto-completion suggestions. This capability is advertised to clients during the initialize handshake.
The primary function of this capability is to improve the user experience by offering contextual suggestions for argument values or resource identifiers based on partial input.
See the schema for details.

This class is intentionally empty as the Model Context Protocol specification does not currently define additional properties for completions capabilities. Future versions of the specification might extend this capability with additional configuration options.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock
member.summary: Represents content within the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

remarks.para: The class is a fundamental type in the MCP that can represent different forms of content based on the property. Derived types like , , and provide the type-specific content.
This class is used throughout the MCP for representing content in messages, tool responses, and other communication between clients and servers.
See the schema for more details.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.#ctor
member.summary: Prevent external derivations.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type
member.summary: When overridden in a derived class, gets the type of content.
member.value: The type of content. Valid values include "image", "audio", "text", "resource", "resource_link", "tool_use", and "tool_result".
member.remarks: This value determines the structure of the content object.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Annotations
member.summary: Gets or sets optional annotations for the content.
member.remarks: These annotations can be used to specify the intended audience (, , or both) and the priority level of the content. Clients can use this information to filter or prioritize content for different roles.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Meta
member.summary: Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
member.remarks: Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Converter
member.summary: Provides a for .
member.remarks: Provides a polymorphic converter for the class that doesn't require setting explicitly.

member.summary: Represents text provided to or from an LLM.

member.remarks: This method stores the provided bytes as and lazily encodes them to base64 UTF-8 bytes for .

is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.

remarks.para: When getting, this member will decode the value in and cache the result. Subsequent accesses return the cached value unless is modified.

member.summary: Gets or sets the MIME type (or "media type") of the content, specifying the format of the data.

The MIME type of the audio.

member.remarks: Setting this value will invalidate any cached value of .

member.summary: Gets the decoded audio data represented by .

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock
member.summary: Represents the contents of a resource, embedded into a prompt or tool call result.
member.remarks: It is up to the client how best to render embedded resources for the benefit of the LLM and/or the user.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock.Resource
member.summary: Gets or sets the resource content of the message when is "resource".

remarks.para: Resources can be either text-based () or binary (), allowing for flexible data representation. Each resource has a URI that can be used for identification and retrieval.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock
member.summary: Represents a resource that the server is capable of reading, included in a prompt or tool call result.
member.remarks: Resource links returned by tools are not guaranteed to appear in the results of `resources/list` requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Uri
member.summary: Gets or sets the URI of this resource.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets a human-readable name for this resource.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Title
member.summary: Gets or sets a title for this resource.
member.remarks: This is intended for UI and end-user contexts. It is optimized to be human-readable and easily understood, even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology. If not provided, can be used for display.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets a description of what this resource represents.

remarks.para: This description can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a \"hint\" to the model.
The description should provide clear context about the resource's content, format, and purpose. This helps AI models make better decisions about when to access or reference the resource.
Client applications can also use this description for display purposes in user interfaces or to help users understand the available resources.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.MimeType
member.summary: Gets or sets the MIME type of this resource.

remarks.para: specifies the format of the resource content, helping clients to properly interpret and display the data. Common MIME types include "text/plain" for plain text, "application/pdf" for PDF documents, "image/png" for PNG images, and "application/json" for JSON data.
This property can be if the MIME type is unknown or not applicable for the resource.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Size
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the raw resource content (before base64 encoding), in bytes, if known.
member.remarks: This value can be used by applications to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Icons
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this resource.
member.remarks: This can be used by clients to display the resource's icon in a user interface.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock
member.summary: Represents a request from the assistant to call a tool.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock.Id
member.summary: Gets or sets a unique identifier for this tool use.
member.remarks: This ID is used to match tool results to their corresponding tool uses.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the tool to call.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock.Input
member.summary: Gets or sets the arguments to pass to the tool, conforming to the tool's input schema.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock
member.summary: Represents the result of a tool use, provided by the user back to the assistant.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.ToolUseId
member.summary: Gets or sets the ID of the tool use this result corresponds to.
member.remarks: This value must match the ID from a previous .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.Content
member.summary: Gets or sets the unstructured result content of the tool use.
member.remarks: This value has the same format as CallToolResult.Content and can include text, images, audio, resource links, and embedded resources.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.StructuredContent
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional structured result object.
member.remarks: If the tool defined an outputSchema, this object should conform to that schema.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.IsError
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool use resulted in an error.
member.value: if the tool use resulted in an error; if it succeeded. The default is .
member.remarks: If , the content typically describes the error that occurred.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion
member.summary: Specifies the context inclusion options for a request in the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

remarks.para: See the schema for details.
, and in particular and , are deprecated. Servers should only use these values if the client declares with set. These values might be removed in future spec releases.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.None
member.summary: No context should be included.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.ThisServer
member.summary: Context from the server that sent the request should be included.
member.remarks: This value is soft-deprecated. Servers should only use this value if the client declares ClientCapabilities.Sampling.Context.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.AllServers
member.summary: Context from all servers that the client is connected to should be included.
member.remarks: This value is soft-deprecated. Servers should only use this value if the client declares ClientCapabilities.Sampling.Context.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a server to sample an LLM via the client.
member.remarks: See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.IncludeContext
member.summary: Gets or sets an indication as to which server contexts should be included in the prompt.

remarks.para: The client might ignore this request.
, and in particular and , are deprecated. Servers should only use these values if the client declares with set. These values might be removed in future spec releases.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.MaxTokens
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of tokens to generate in the LLM response, as requested by the server.

remarks.para: A token is generally a word or part of a word in the text. Setting this value helps control response length and computation time. The client can choose to sample fewer tokens than requested.
The client must respect the parameter.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Messages
member.summary: Gets or sets the messages requested by the server to be included in the prompt.
member.remarks: The list of messages in a sampling request should not be retained between separate requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Metadata
member.summary: Gets or sets optional metadata to pass through to the LLM provider.

remarks.para: The format of this metadata is provider-specific and can include model-specific settings or configuration that isn't covered by standard parameters. This allows for passing custom parameters that are specific to certain AI models or providers.
The client may modify or ignore metadata.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.ModelPreferences
member.summary: Gets or sets the server's preferences for which model to select.

remarks.para: The client might ignore these preferences.
These preferences help the client make an appropriate model selection based on the server's priorities for cost, speed, intelligence, and specific model hints.

When multiple dimensions are specified (cost, speed, intelligence), the client should balance these based on their relative values. If specific model hints are provided, the client should evaluate them in order and prioritize them over numeric priorities.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.StopSequences
member.summary: Gets or sets optional sequences of characters that signal the LLM to stop generating text when encountered.

remarks.para: When the model generates any of these sequences during sampling, text generation stops immediately, even if the maximum token limit hasn't been reached. This behavior is useful for controlling generation endings or preventing the model from continuing beyond certain points.

Stop sequences are typically case-sensitive, and the LLM will only stop generation when a produced sequence exactly matches one of the provided sequences. Common uses include ending markers like "END", punctuation like ".", or special delimiter sequences like "###".
The client may modify or ignore stop sequences.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.SystemPrompt
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional system prompt the server wants to use for sampling.
member.remarks: The client might modify or omit this prompt.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Temperature
member.summary: Gets or sets the temperature to use for sampling, as requested by the server.

remarks.para: Temperature controls randomness in model responses. Higher values produce higher randomness, and lower values produce more stable output. The valid range depends on the model provider.
The client may modify or ignore this value.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Tools
member.summary: Gets or sets tools that the model can use during generation.
member.remarks: The tool definitions in this array are scoped to this sampling request. They do not need to correspond to tools registered on the server via .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.ToolChoice
member.summary: Gets or sets controls for how the model uses tools.
member.remarks: This controls whether and how the model uses the request-scoped during sampling.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Task
member.summary: Gets or sets optional task metadata to augment this request with task execution.
member.remarks: When present, indicates that the requestor wants this operation executed as a task. The receiver must support task augmentation for this specific request type.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult
member.summary: Represents a client's response to a from the server.
member.remarks: See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.Content
member.summary: Gets or sets the content of the assistant's response.
member.remarks: In the corresponding JSON, this might be a single content block or an array of content blocks.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.Model
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the model that generated the message.

remarks.para: This value should contain the specific model identifier such as "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022" or "o3-mini".
This property allows the server to know which model was used to generate the response, enabling appropriate handling based on the model's capabilities and characteristics.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReason
member.summary: Gets or sets the reason why message generation (sampling) stopped, if known.
member.remarks:
Standard values include:
endTurnThe participant is yielding the conversation to the other party.
maxTokensThe response was truncated due to reaching token limits.
stopSequenceA specific stop sequence was encountered during generation.
toolUseThe model wants to use one or more tools.
This field is an open string to allow for provider-specific stop reasons.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.Role
member.summary: Gets or sets the role of the user who generated the message.
member.value: The role of the user who generated the message. The default is .

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonEndTurn
member.summary: The stop reason "endTurn".

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonMaxTokens
member.summary: The stop reason "maxTokens".

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonStopSequence
member.summary: The stop reason "stopSequence".

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonToolUse
member.summary: The stop reason "toolUse".

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateTaskResult
member.summary: Represents the response to a task-augmented request.

remarks.para: When a client sends a request with a task parameter, the server immediately returns a containing the created task information instead of the normal result type. The actual result can be retrieved later via tasks/result.
This type is returned for any task-augmented request including tools/call, sampling/createMessage, and elicitation/create.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateTaskResult.Task
member.summary: Gets or sets the task data for the newly created task.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.DebuggerDisplayHelper
member.summary: Internal helper methods for DebuggerDisplay implementations.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.DebuggerDisplayHelper.GetBase64LengthDisplay(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the decoded length of base64 data for debugger display.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.DebuggerDisplayHelper.GetBase64LengthDisplay(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte})
member.summary: Gets the decoded length of base64 data encoded as UTF-8 bytes for debugger display.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability
member.summary: Represents the capability for a client to provide server-requested additional information during interactions.

remarks.para: This capability enables the MCP client to respond to elicitation requests from an MCP server. Clients must support at least one elicitation mode: form (in-band) or url (out-of-band via URL).
When this capability is enabled, an MCP server can request the client to provide additional information during interactions. The client must set a to process these requests.

Two modes of elicitation are supported:
form: In-band elicitation where data is collected via a form and exposed to the client
url: URL mode (out-of-band) elicitation via navigation where sensitive data is not exposed to the client

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Form
member.summary: Gets or sets the form mode elicitation (in-band) capability, indicating support for in-band elicitation.
member.remarks: When present, indicates the client supports form mode elicitation where structured data is collected through a form interface and returned to the server.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL mode (out-of-band) elicitation capability.
member.remarks: When present, indicates the client supports URL mode elicitation for secure out-of-band interactions such as OAuth flows, payments, or collecting sensitive credentials.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Converter
member.summary: Provides a converter that normalizes blank capability objects to imply form support.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCompleteNotificationParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a notification emitted after a URL-mode elicitation finishes out-of-band.

remarks.para: The payload references the original elicitation by ID so that clients can resume deferred requests or update pending UI once the external flow completes.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCompleteNotificationParams.ElicitationId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier of the elicitation that completed.
member.remarks: This matches from the originating request and allows clients to correlate the completion notification with previously issued prompts.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams
member.summary: Represents a message issued from the server to elicit additional information from the user via the client.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Mode
member.summary: Gets or sets the elicitation mode: "form" for in-band data collection or "url" for out-of-band URL navigation.

item.description: form: Client collects structured data via a form interface. Data is exposed to the client.
url: Client navigates user to a URL for out-of-band interaction. Sensitive data is not exposed to the client.

member.exception: The value is not "form" or "url".

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.ElicitationId
member.summary: Gets or sets a unique identifier for this elicitation request.

remarks.para: Used to track and correlate the elicitation across multiple messages, especially for out-of-band flows that may complete asynchronously.
Required for url mode elicitation to enable progress tracking and completion detection.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL to navigate to for out-of-band elicitation.

remarks.para: Required when is "url". The client should prompt the user for consent and then navigate to this URL in a user-agent (browser) where the user completes the required interaction.
URLs must not appear in any other field of the elicitation request for security reasons.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets the message to present to the user.
member.remarks: For form mode, this describes what information is being requested. For url mode, this explains why the user needs to navigate to the URL.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestedSchema
member.summary: Gets or sets the requested schema for form mode elicitation.
member.remarks: Only applicable when is "form".
member.value: Possible values are , , , , , , , and (deprecated).

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Task
member.summary: Gets or sets optional task metadata to augment this request with task execution.
member.remarks: When present, indicates that the requestor wants this operation executed as a task. The receiver must support task augmentation for this specific request type.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestSchema
member.summary: Represents a request schema used in a form mode elicitation request.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestSchema.Type
member.summary: Gets the type of the schema.
member.remarks: This value is always "object".

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestSchema.Properties
member.summary: Gets or sets the properties of the schema.

member.summary: Gets or sets the list of allowed string values for the enum.
