member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.IBaseMetadata
member.summary: Provides a base interface for metadata with name (identifier) and title (display name) properties.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.IBaseMetadata.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this item.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.IBaseMetadata.Title
member.summary: Gets or sets a title.
member.remarks: This is intended for UI and end-user contexts. It is optimized to be human-readable and easily understood, even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology. If not provided, can be used for display (except for tools, where , if present, should be given precedence over using ).

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon
member.summary: Represents an icon that can be used to visually identify an implementation, resource, tool, or prompt.

remarks.para: Icons enhance user interfaces by providing visual context and improving the discoverability of available functionality. Each icon includes a source URI pointing to the icon resource, and optional MIME type and size information.
Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:

item.description: image/png - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
image/jpeg (and image/jpg) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

remarks.para: Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:

item.description: image/svg+xml - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
image/webp - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

remarks.para: See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.Source
member.summary: Gets or sets the URI pointing to the icon resource.

remarks.para: This value can be an HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to an image file or a data URI with base64-encoded image data.
Consumers SHOULD take steps to ensure URLs serving icons are from the same domain as the client/server or a trusted domain.
Consumers SHOULD take appropriate precautions when consuming SVGs as they can contain executable JavaScript.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.MimeType
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional MIME type of the icon.
member.remarks: This value can be used to override the server's MIME type if it's missing or generic. Common values include "image/png", "image/jpeg", "image/svg+xml", and "image/webp".

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.Sizes
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional size specifications for the icon.

remarks.para: This property can specify one or more sizes at which the icon file can be used. Examples include "48x48", "any" for scalable formats like SVG.
If not provided, clients should assume that the icon can be used at any size.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.Theme
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional theme for this icon.
member.remarks: may be "light" or "dark". "light" indicates the icon is designed to be used with a light background, and "dark" indicates the icon is designed to be used with a dark background. If not provided, clients should assume the icon can be used with any theme.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation
member.summary: Provides the name and version of an MCP implementation.

remarks.para: The class is used to identify MCP clients and servers during the initialization handshake. It provides version and name information that can be used for compatibility checks, logging, and debugging.
Both clients and servers provide this information during connection establishment.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Version
member.summary: Gets or sets the version of the implementation.
member.remarks: The version is used during client-server handshake to identify implementation versions, which can be important for troubleshooting compatibility issues or when reporting bugs.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional description of the implementation.

remarks.para: This description helps users and developers understand what the implementation provides and its purpose. It should clearly explain the functionality and capabilities offered.
The description is typically used in documentation, UI displays, and for providing context to users about the server or client they are interacting with.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Icons
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this implementation.
member.remarks: This value can be used by clients to display the implementation's icon in a user interface.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.WebsiteUrl
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL of the website for this implementation.

remarks.para: This URL can be used by clients to link to documentation or more information about the implementation.
Consumers SHOULD take steps to ensure URLs are from the same domain as the client/server or a trusted domain to prevent security issues.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializedNotificationParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a sent from the client to the server after initialization has finished.
member.remarks: See the schema for details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request sent by a client to a server during the protocol handshake.

remarks.para: The is the first message sent in the Model Context Protocol communication flow. It establishes the connection between client and server, negotiates the protocol version, and declares the client's capabilities.
After sending this request, the client should wait for an response before sending an notification to complete the handshake.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams.ProtocolVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the version of the Model Context Protocol that the client wants to use.

remarks.para: Protocol version is specified using a date-based versioning scheme in the format "YYYY-MM-DD". The client and server must agree on a protocol version to communicate successfully.
During initialization, the server will check if it supports this requested version. If there's a mismatch, the server will reject the connection with a version mismatch error.
See the protocol specification for version details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams.Capabilities
member.summary: Gets or sets the client's capabilities.
member.remarks: Capabilities define the features the client supports, such as "sampling" or "roots".

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams.ClientInfo
member.summary: Gets or sets information about the client implementation, including its name and version.
member.remarks: This information is required during the initialization handshake to identify the client. Servers might use this information for logging, debugging, or compatibility checks.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a request sent to the server during connection establishment.

remarks.para: The is sent by the server in response to an message from the client. It contains information about the server, its capabilities, and the protocol version that will be used for the session.
After receiving this response, the client should send an notification to complete the handshake.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.ProtocolVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the version of the Model Context Protocol that the server will use for this session.

remarks.para: This is the protocol version the server has agreed to use, which should match the client's requested version. If there's a mismatch, the client should throw an exception to prevent communication issues due to incompatible protocol versions.
The protocol uses a date-based versioning scheme in the format "YYYY-MM-DD".
See the protocol specification for version details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.Capabilities
member.summary: Gets or sets the server's capabilities.
member.remarks: This property defines the features the server supports, such as "tools", "prompts", "resources", or "logging", and other protocol-specific functionality.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.ServerInfo
member.summary: Gets or sets information about the server implementation, including its name and version.
member.remarks: This information identifies the server during the initialization handshake. Clients might use this information for logging, debugging, or compatibility checks.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.Instructions
member.summary: Gets or sets optional instructions for using the server and its features.

remarks.para: These instructions should focus on guidance that helps a model use the server effectively, such as workflow tips, capability relationships, and server-specific conventions. They should avoid repeating tool descriptions, prompt descriptions, or resource descriptions that are already available through other protocol responses.
Client applications often use these instructions as system messages for LLM interactions to provide context about available functionality.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport
member.summary: Represents a transport mechanism for MCP (Model Context Protocol) communication between clients and servers.

remarks.para: The interface is the core abstraction for bidirectional communication. It provides methods for sending and receiving messages, abstracting away the underlying transport mechanism, and allowing protocol implementations to be decoupled from communication details.
Implementations of handle the serialization, transmission, and reception of messages over various channels like standard input/output streams and HTTP (Server-Sent Events).
While is responsible for establishing a client's connection, represents an established session. Client implementations typically obtain an instance by calling .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport.SessionId
member.summary: Gets an identifier associated with the current MCP session.
member.remarks: The identifier is typically populated in transports supporting multiple sessions, such as Streamable HTTP or SSE. This property can return if the session hasn't initialized or if the transport doesn't support multiple sessions (as is the case with STDIO).

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport.MessageReader
member.summary: Gets a channel reader for receiving messages from the transport.

remarks.para: The provides access to incoming JSON-RPC messages received by the transport. It returns a which allows messages to be consumed in a thread-safe manner.

The reader will continue to provide messages as long as the transport is connected. When the transport is disconnected or disposed, the channel will be completed and no more messages will be available after any already transmitted messages are consumed.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport.SendMessageAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a JSON-RPC message through the transport.
member.param: The JSON-RPC message to send.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous send operation.
member.exception: The transport is not connected.

remarks.para: This method serializes and sends the provided JSON-RPC message through the transport connection.

This is a core method used by higher-level abstractions in the MCP protocol implementation. Most client code should use the higher-level methods provided by , , or , rather than accessing this method directly.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError
member.summary: Represents an error response message in the JSON-RPC protocol.

remarks.para: Error responses are sent when a request cannot be fulfilled or encounters an error during processing. Like successful responses, error messages include the same ID as the original request, allowing the sender to match errors with their corresponding requests.
Each error response contains a structured error detail object with a numeric code, descriptive message, and optional additional data to provide more context about the error.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError.Error
member.summary: Gets or sets detailed error information for the failed request, containing an error code, message, and optional additional data.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail
member.summary: Represents detailed error information for JSON-RPC error responses.
member.remarks: This class is used as part of the message to provide structured error information when a request cannot be fulfilled. The JSON-RPC 2.0 specification defines a standard format for error responses that includes a numeric code, a human-readable message, and optional additional data.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail.Code
member.summary: Gets or sets an integer error code according to the JSON-RPC specification.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail.Message
member.summary: Gets or sets a short description of the error.
member.remarks: This description is expected to be a brief, human-readable explanation of what went wrong. For standard error codes, it's recommended to use the descriptions defined in the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail.Data
member.summary: Gets or sets optional additional error data.
member.remarks: This property can contain any additional information that might help the client understand or resolve the error. Common examples include validation errors, stack traces (in development environments), or contextual information about the error condition.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage
member.summary: Represents any JSON-RPC message used in the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
member.remarks: This interface serves as the foundation for all message types in the JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol used by MCP, including requests, responses, notifications, and errors. JSON-RPC is a stateless, lightweight remote procedure call (RPC) protocol that uses JSON as its data format.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.#ctor
member.summary: Prevent external derivations.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.JsonRpc
member.summary: Gets or sets the JSON-RPC protocol version used.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Context
member.summary: Gets or sets the contextual information for this JSON-RPC message.

remarks.para: This property contains transport-specific and runtime context information that accompanies JSON-RPC messages but is not serialized as part of the JSON-RPC payload. This includes transport references, execution context, and authenticated user information.
This property should only be set when implementing a custom that needs to pass additional per-message context or to pass a to or .

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Converter
member.summary: Provides a for messages, handling polymorphic deserialization of different message types.

remarks.para: This converter is responsible for correctly deserializing JSON-RPC messages into their appropriate concrete types based on the message structure. It analyzes the JSON payload and determines if it represents a request, notification, successful response, or error response.

The type determination rules follow the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification:
Messages with "method" and "id" properties are deserialized as .
Messages with "method" but no "id" property are deserialized as .
Messages with "id" and "result" properties are deserialized as .
Messages with "id" and "error" properties are deserialized as .

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext
member.summary: Contains contextual information for JSON-RPC messages that is not part of the JSON-RPC protocol specification.
member.remarks: This class holds transport-specific and runtime context information that accompanies JSON-RPC messages but is not serialized as part of the JSON-RPC payload. This includes transport references, execution context, and authenticated user information.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.RelatedTransport
member.summary: Gets or sets the transport the was received on or should be sent over.
member.remarks: This property is used to support the Streamable HTTP transport where the specification states that the server SHOULD include JSON-RPC responses in the HTTP response body for the POST request containing the corresponding JSON-RPC request. It can be for other transports.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.ExecutionContext
member.summary: Gets or sets the that should be used to run any handlers.
member.remarks: This property is used to support the Streamable HTTP transport in its default stateful mode. In this mode, the outlives the initial HTTP request context it was created on, and new JSON-RPC messages can originate from future HTTP requests. This behavior allows the transport to flow the context with the JSON-RPC message. This is particularly useful for enabling IHttpContextAccessor in tool calls.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.User
member.summary: Gets or sets the authenticated user associated with this JSON-RPC message.

remarks.para: This property contains the representing the authenticated user who initiated this JSON-RPC message. This enables request handlers to access user identity and authorization information without requiring dependency on HTTP context accessors or other HTTP-specific abstractions.

The user information is automatically populated by the transport layer when processing incoming HTTP requests in ASP.NET Core scenarios. For other transport types or scenarios where user authentication is not applicable, this property can be .

This property is particularly useful in the Streamable HTTP transport where JSON-RPC messages might outlive the original HTTP request context, allowing user identity to be preserved throughout the message processing pipeline.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.Items
member.summary: Gets or sets a key/value collection that can be used to share data within the scope of this message.

remarks.para: This property allows data to be flowed throughout the message processing pipeline, including from incoming message filters to request-specific filters and handlers.
When creating a or for server-side processing, the Items dictionary from this context will be used, ensuring data set in message filters is available in request filters and handlers.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageWithId
member.summary: Represents a JSON-RPC message used in the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and that includes an ID.
member.remarks: In the JSON-RPC protocol, messages with an ID require a response from the receiver. This includes request messages (which expect a matching response) and response messages (which include the ID of the original request they're responding to). The ID is used to correlate requests with their responses, allowing asynchronous communication where multiple requests can be sent without waiting for responses.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageWithId.#ctor
member.summary: Prevent external derivations.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageWithId.Id
member.summary: Gets or sets the message identifier.
member.remarks: Each ID is expected to be unique within the context of a given session.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification
member.summary: Represents a notification message in the JSON-RPC protocol.
member.remarks: Notifications are messages that do not require a response and are not matched with a response message. They are useful for one-way communication, such as log notifications and progress updates. Unlike requests, notifications do not include an ID field, since there will be no response to match with it.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification.Method
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the notification method.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification.Params
member.summary: Gets or sets optional parameters for the notification.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest
member.summary: Represents a request message in the JSON-RPC protocol.
member.remarks:
Requests are messages that require a response from the receiver. Each request includes a unique ID that will be included in the corresponding response message (either a success response or an error).
The receiver of a request message is expected to execute the specified method with the provided parameters and return either a with the result, or a if the method execution fails.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest.Method
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the method to invoke.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest.Params
member.summary: Gets or sets optional parameters for the method.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcResponse
member.summary: Represents a successful response message in the JSON-RPC protocol.

remarks.para: Response messages are sent in reply to a request message and contain the result of the method execution. Each response includes the same ID as the original request, allowing the sender to match responses with their corresponding requests.
This class represents a successful response with a result. For error responses, see .

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcResponse.Result
member.summary: Gets or sets the result of the method invocation.
member.remarks: This property contains the result data returned by the server in response to the JSON-RPC method request.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListPromptsRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a client to request a list of prompts available from the server.
member.remarks: The server responds with a containing the available prompts. See the schema for details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListPromptsResult
member.summary: Represents a server's response to a request from the client, containing available prompts.

remarks.para: This result is returned when a client sends a request to discover available prompts on the server.

It inherits from , allowing for paginated responses when there are many prompts. The server can provide the property to indicate there are more prompts available beyond what was returned in the current response.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListPromptsResult.Prompts
member.summary: Gets or sets a list of prompts or prompt templates that the server offers.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourcesRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a client to request a list of resources available from the server.
member.remarks: The server responds with a containing the available resources. See the schema for details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourcesResult
member.summary: Represents a server's response to a request from the client, containing available resources.

remarks.para: This result is returned when a client sends a request to discover available resources on the server.

It inherits from , allowing for paginated responses when there are many resources. The server can provide the property to indicate there are more resources available beyond what was returned in the current response.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourcesResult.Resources
member.summary: Gets or sets a list of resources that the server offers.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourceTemplatesRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a client to request a list of resource templates available from the server.
member.remarks: The server responds with a containing the available resource templates. See the schema for details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourceTemplatesResult
member.summary: Represents a server's response to a request from the client, containing available resource templates.

remarks.para: This result is returned when a client sends a request to discover available resource templates on the server.

It inherits from , allowing for paginated responses when there are many resource templates. The server can provide the property to indicate there are more resource templates available beyond what was returned in the current response.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourceTemplatesResult.ResourceTemplates
member.summary: Gets or sets a list of resource templates that the server offers.
member.remarks: This collection contains all the resource templates returned in the current page of results. Each provides metadata about resources available on the server, including URI templates, names, descriptions, and MIME types.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListRootsRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a server to request a list of roots available from the client.
member.remarks: The client responds with a containing the client's roots. See the schema for details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListRootsResult
member.summary: Represents a client's response to a request from the server, containing available roots.

remarks.para: This result is returned when a server sends a request to discover available roots on the client.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListRootsResult.Roots
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of root URIs provided by the client.
member.remarks: This collection contains all available root URIs and their associated metadata. Each root serves as an entry point for resource navigation in the Model Context Protocol.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters for a tasks/list request to retrieve a list of tasks.
member.remarks: This operation supports cursor-based pagination. Receivers should use cursor-based pagination to limit the number of tasks returned in a single response.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a tasks/list request.
member.remarks: The result contains an array of task objects and an optional cursor for pagination. If is present, more tasks are available.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksResult.Tasks
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of tasks.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a client to request a list of tools available from the server.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult
member.summary: Represents a server's response to a request from the client, containing available tools.

remarks.para: This result is returned when a client sends a request to discover available tools on the server.

It inherits from , allowing for paginated responses when there are many tools. The server can provide the property to indicate there are more tools available beyond what was returned in the current response.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult.Tools
member.summary: Gets or sets the server's response to a tools/list request from the client.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingCapability
member.summary: Represents the logging capability configuration for a Model Context Protocol server.

remarks.para: This capability allows clients to set the logging level and receive log messages from the server. See the schema for details.

This class is intentionally empty as the Model Context Protocol specification does not currently define additional properties for logging capabilities. Future versions of the specification may extend this capability with additional configuration options.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel
member.summary: Indicates the severity of a log message.
member.remarks: These values map to syslog message severities, as specified in RFC-5424.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Debug
member.summary: Detailed debug information, typically only valuable to developers.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Info
member.summary: Normal operational messages that require no action.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Notice
member.summary: Normal but significant events that might deserve attention.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Warning
member.summary: Warning conditions that don't represent an error but indicate potential issues.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Error
member.summary: Error conditions that should be addressed but don't require immediate action.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Critical
member.summary: Critical conditions that require immediate attention.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Alert
member.summary: Action must be taken immediately to address the condition.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Emergency
member.summary: System is unusable and requires immediate attention.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a notification sent whenever a log message is generated.

remarks.para: Logging notifications allow servers to communicate diagnostic information to clients with varying severity levels. Clients can filter these messages based on the and properties.
If no request has been sent from the client, the server can decide which messages to send automatically.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Level
member.summary: Gets or sets the severity of this log message.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Logger
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional name of the logger issuing this message.

remarks.para: typically represents a category or component in the server's logging system. The logger name is useful for filtering and routing log messages in client applications.
When implementing custom servers, choose clear, hierarchical logger names to help clients understand the source of log messages.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Data
member.summary: Gets or sets the data to be logged, such as a string message or an object. Any JSON serializable type is allowed here.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask
member.summary: Represents an MCP task, which is a durable state machine carrying information about the underlying execution state of a request.

remarks.para: Tasks are useful for representing expensive computations and batch processing requests. Each task is uniquely identifiable by a receiver-generated task ID.
Tasks follow a defined lifecycle through the property. They begin in the status and may transition through various states before reaching a terminal status (, , or ).
See the tasks specification for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.TaskId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for the task.
member.remarks: Task IDs are generated by the receiver when creating a task and must be unique among all tasks controlled by that receiver.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.Status
member.summary: Gets or sets the current state of the task execution.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.StatusMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional human-readable message describing the current state.
member.remarks: This message can be present for any status, including error details for failed tasks.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.CreatedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.
member.remarks: Receivers must include this timestamp in all task responses to indicate when the task was created.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.LastUpdatedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task status was last updated.
member.remarks: Receivers must include this timestamp in all task responses to indicate when the task was last updated.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.TimeToLive
member.summary: Gets or sets the time to live (retention duration) from creation before the task may be deleted.

remarks.para: A null value indicates unlimited lifetime. After a task's TTL lifetime has elapsed, receivers may delete the task and its results, regardless of the task status.
Receivers may override the requested TTL duration and must include the actual TTL duration (or null for unlimited) in task responses.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.PollInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the suggested time between status checks.
member.remarks: Requestors should respect this value when provided to avoid excessive polling. This value is optional and may not be present in all task responses.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata
member.summary: Represents metadata for augmenting a request with task execution.

remarks.para: When included in a request's params, this metadata signals that the requestor wants the receiver to execute the request as a task rather than synchronously. The receiver will return a containing task data instead of the actual operation result.
Requestors can specify a desired TTL (time-to-live) duration for the task, though receivers may override this value based on their resource management policies.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata.TimeToLive
member.summary: Gets or sets the requested time to live (retention duration) to retain the task from creation.

remarks.para: This is a hint to the receiver about how long the requestor expects to need access to the task data. Receivers may override this value based on their resource constraints and policies.
A null value indicates no specific retention requirement. The actual TTL used by the receiver will be returned in the property.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents the tasks capability configuration for servers and clients.

remarks.para: The tasks capability enables requestors (clients or servers) to augment their requests with tasks for long-running operations. Tasks are durable state machines that carry information about the underlying execution state of requests.

During initialization, both parties exchange their tasks capabilities to establish which operations support task-based execution. Requestors should only augment requests with a task if the corresponding capability has been declared by the receiver.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability.List
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this party supports the tasks/list operation.
member.remarks: When present, indicates support for listing all tasks.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability.Cancel
member.summary: Gets or sets whether this party supports the tasks/cancel operation.
member.remarks: When present, indicates support for cancelling tasks.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability.Requests
member.summary: Gets or sets which request types support task augmentation.

remarks.para: The set of capabilities in this property is exhaustive. If a request type is not present, it does not support task augmentation.
For servers, this typically includes tools/call. For clients, this typically includes sampling/createMessage and elicitation/create.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents task support for tool-specific requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability.Tools
member.summary: Gets or sets task support for tool-related requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability.Sampling
member.summary: Gets or sets task support for sampling-related requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability.Elicitation
member.summary: Gets or sets task support for elicitation-related requests.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolsMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents task support for tool-related requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolsMcpTasksCapability.Call
member.summary: Gets or sets whether tools/call requests support task augmentation.
member.remarks: When present, indicates that the server supports task-augmented tools/call requests.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents task support for sampling-related requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMcpTasksCapability.CreateMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets whether sampling/createMessage requests support task augmentation.
member.remarks: When present, indicates that the client supports task-augmented sampling/createMessage requests.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents task support for elicitation-related requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationMcpTasksCapability.Create
member.summary: Gets or sets whether elicitation/create requests support task augmentation.
member.remarks: When present, indicates that the client supports task-augmented elicitation/create requests.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents the capability for listing tasks.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents the capability for cancelling tasks.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents the capability for task-augmented tools/call requests.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents the capability for task-augmented sampling/createMessage requests.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateElicitationMcpTasksCapability
member.summary: Represents the capability for task-augmented elicitation/create requests.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus
member.summary: Represents the status of an MCP task.

remarks.para:
Tasks progress through a defined lifecycle:
: The request is currently being processed.
: The receiver needs input from the requestor. The requestor should call tasks/result to receive input requests.
: The request completed successfully and results are available.
: The request did not complete successfully.
: The request was cancelled before completion.
Terminal states are , , and . Once a task reaches a terminal state, it cannot transition to any other status.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working
member.summary: The request is currently being processed.
member.remarks: Tasks begin in this status when created. From , tasks may transition to , , , or .

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.InputRequired
member.summary: The receiver needs input from the requestor.
member.remarks: The requestor should call tasks/result to receive input requests, even though the task has not reached a terminal state. From , tasks may transition to , , , or .

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed
member.summary: The request completed successfully and results are available.
member.remarks: This is a terminal status. Tasks in this status cannot transition to any other status.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed
member.summary: The associated request did not complete successfully.
member.remarks: This is a terminal status. For tool calls specifically, this includes cases where the tool call result has isError set to true. Tasks in this status cannot transition to any other status.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled
member.summary: The request was cancelled before completion.
member.remarks: This is a terminal status. Tasks in this status cannot transition to any other status.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters for a notifications/tasks/status notification.

remarks.para: When a task status changes, receivers may send this notification to inform the requestor of the change. This notification includes the full task state.

Requestors must not rely on receiving this notification, as it is optional. Receivers are not required to send status notifications and may choose to only send them for certain status transitions. Requestors should continue to poll via tasks/get to ensure they receive status updates.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.TaskId
member.summary: Gets or sets the task ID.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.Status
member.summary: Gets or sets the current status of the task.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.StatusMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional human-readable message describing the current state.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.CreatedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.LastUpdatedAt
member.summary: Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task status was last updated.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.TimeToLive
member.summary: Gets or sets the time to live (retention duration) from creation before the task may be deleted.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.PollInterval
member.summary: Gets or sets the suggested time between status checks.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelHint
member.summary: Provides hints to use for model selection.

remarks.para: When multiple hints are specified in , they are evaluated in order, with the first match taking precedence. Clients should prioritize these hints over numeric priorities.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelHint.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets a hint for a model name.
member.remarks: The specified string can be a partial or full model name. Clients can also map hints to equivalent models from different providers. Clients make the final model selection based on these preferences and their available models.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences
member.summary: Represents a server's preferences for model selection, requested of the client during sampling.

remarks.para: Because LLMs can vary along multiple dimensions, choosing the "best" model is rarely straightforward. Different models excel in different areas—some are faster but less capable, others are more capable but more expensive, and so on. This class allows servers to express their priorities across multiple dimensions to help clients make an appropriate selection for their use case.
These preferences are always advisory. The client may ignore them. It is also up to the client to decide how to interpret these preferences and how to balance them against other considerations.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.CostPriority
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates how much to prioritize cost when selecting a model.
member.remarks: A value of 0 means cost is not important, while a value of 1 means cost is the most important factor.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.Hints
member.summary: Gets or sets optional hints to use for model selection.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.SpeedPriority
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates how much to prioritize sampling speed (latency) when selecting a model.
member.remarks: A value of 0 means speed is not important, while a value of 1 means speed is the most important factor.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.IntelligencePriority
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates how much to prioritize intelligence and capabilities when selecting a model.
member.remarks: A value of 0 means intelligence is not important, while a value of 1 means intelligence is the most important factor.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods
member.summary: Provides constants with the names of common notification methods used in the MCP protocol.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ToolListChangedNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent by a server when the list of available tools changes.
member.remarks: This notification informs clients that the set of available tools has been modified. Changes may include tools being added, removed, or updated. Upon receiving this notification, clients may refresh their tool list by calling the appropriate method to get the updated list of tools.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.PromptListChangedNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent by the server when the list of available prompts changes.
member.remarks: This notification informs clients that the set of available prompts has been modified. Changes may include prompts being added, removed, or updated. Upon receiving this notification, clients may refresh their prompt list by calling the appropriate method to get the updated list of prompts.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ResourceListChangedNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent by the server when the list of available resources changes.
member.remarks: This notification informs clients that the set of available resources has been modified. Changes may include resources being added, removed, or updated. Upon receiving this notification, clients may refresh their resource list by calling the appropriate method to get the updated list of resources.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ResourceUpdatedNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent by the server when a resource is updated.
member.remarks: This notification is used to inform clients about changes to a specific resource they have subscribed to. When a resource is updated, the server sends this notification to all clients that have subscribed to that resource.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.RootsListChangedNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent by the client when roots have been updated.

remarks.para: This notification informs the server that the client's "roots" have changed. Roots define the boundaries of where servers can operate within the filesystem, allowing them to understand which directories and files they have access to. Servers can request the list of roots from supporting clients and receive notifications when that list changes.
After receiving this notification, servers can refresh their knowledge of roots by calling the appropriate method to get the updated list of roots from the client.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.LoggingMessageNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent by the server when a log message is generated.

remarks.para: This notification is used by the server to send log messages to clients. Log messages can include different severity levels, such as debug, info, warning, or error, and an optional logger name to identify the source component.

The minimum logging level that triggers notifications can be controlled by clients using the request. If no level has been set by a client, the server can determine which messages to send based on its own configuration.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ElicitationCompleteNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent by the server when a URL-mode elicitation flow completes.
member.remarks: This notification references the original elicitation by ID, allowing clients to retry blocked requests or update their UI state once the out-of-band interaction finishes.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.InitializedNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent from the client to the server after initialization has finished.

remarks.para: This notification is sent by the client after it has received and processed the server's response to the request. It signals that the client is ready to begin normal operation and that the initialization phase is complete.
After receiving this notification, the server can begin sending notifications and processing further requests from the client.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent to inform the receiver of a progress update for a long-running request.

remarks.para: This notification provides updates on the progress of long-running operations. It includes a progress token that associates the notification with a specific request, the current progress value, and optionally, a total value and a descriptive message.

Progress notifications can be sent by either the client or the server, depending on the context. Progress notifications enable clients to display progress indicators for operations that might take significant time to complete, such as large file uploads, complex computations, or resource-intensive processing tasks.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.CancelledNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent to indicate that a previously issued request should be canceled.

remarks.para: From the issuer's perspective, the request should still be in-flight. However, due to communication latency, it is always possible that this notification might arrive after the request has already finished.
This notification indicates that the result will be unused, so any associated processing SHOULD cease.
A client must not attempt to cancel its `initialize` request.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.TaskStatusNotification
member.summary: The name of the notification sent when a task status changes.

remarks.para: When a task status changes, receivers may send this notification to inform the requestor of the change. This notification includes the full task state.

Requestors must not rely on receiving this notification, as it is optional. Receivers are not required to send status notifications and may choose to only send them for certain status transitions. Requestors should continue to poll via tasks/get to ensure they receive status updates.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.RelatedTaskMetaKey
member.summary: The metadata key used to associate requests, responses, and notifications with a task.

remarks.para: This constant defines the key "io.modelcontextprotocol/related-task" used in the _meta field to associate messages with their originating task across the entire request lifecycle.
For example, an elicitation that a task-augmented tool call depends on must share the same related task ID with that tool call's task.
For tasks/get, tasks/list, and tasks/cancel operations, this metadata should not be included as the taskId is already present in the message structure.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationParams
member.summary: Provides a base class for notification parameters.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationParams.#ctor
member.summary: Prevent external derivations.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationParams.Meta
member.summary: Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
member.remarks: Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedRequestParams
member.summary: Provides a base class for paginated requests.

member.name: M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedRequestParams.#ctor
member.summary: Prevent external derivations.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedRequestParams.Cursor
member.summary: Gets or sets an opaque token representing the current pagination position.
member.remarks: If provided, the server should return results starting after this cursor. This value should be obtained from the property of a previous request's response.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult
member.summary: Provides a base class for result payloads that support cursor-based pagination.

remarks.para: Pagination allows API responses to be broken into smaller, manageable chunks when there are potentially many results to return or when dynamically computed results might incur measurable latency.
Classes that inherit from implement cursor-based pagination, where the property serves as an opaque token pointing to the next set of results.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult.NextCursor
member.summary: Gets or sets an opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
member.remarks: When a paginated result has more data available, the property will contain a non- token that can be used in subsequent requests to fetch the next page. When there are no more results to return, the property will be .

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PingRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request to verify server connectivity.
member.remarks: The server responds with a . See the schema for details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PingResult
member.summary: Represents the result of a request in the Model Context Protocol.

remarks.para: The is returned in response to a request, which is used to verify that the connection between client and server is still alive and responsive. Since this is a simple connectivity check, the result is an empty object containing no data.
Ping requests can be initiated by either the client or the server to check if the other party is still responsive.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams
member.summary: Represents an out-of-band notification used to inform the receiver of a progress update for a long-running request.
member.remarks: See the schema for more details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams.ProgressToken
member.summary: Gets or sets the progress token that was given in the initial request that's used to associate this notification with the corresponding request.

remarks.para: This token acts as a correlation identifier that links progress updates to their corresponding request.

When an endpoint initiates a request with a in its metadata, the receiver can send progress notifications using this same token. This allows both sides to correlate the notifications with the original request.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams.Progress
member.summary: Gets or sets the progress thus far.
member.remarks: This value should increase for each notification issued as part of the same request, even if the total is unknown.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams.Converter
member.summary: Provides a for .

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt
member.summary: Represents a prompt that the server offers.
member.remarks: See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional description of what this prompt provides.

remarks.para: This description helps developers understand the purpose and use cases for the prompt. It should explain what the prompt is designed to accomplish and any important context.
The description is typically used in documentation, UI displays, and for providing context to client applications that may need to choose between multiple available prompts.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Arguments
member.summary: Gets or sets a list of arguments that this prompt accepts for templating and customization.

remarks.para: This list defines the arguments that can be provided when requesting the prompt. Each argument specifies metadata like name, description, and whether it's required.
When a client makes a request, it can provide values for these arguments that will be substituted into the prompt template or otherwise used to render the prompt.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Icons
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this prompt.
member.remarks: This list can be used by clients to display the prompt's icon in a user interface.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Meta

member.summary: Represents an argument that a prompt can accept for templating and customization.

remarks.para: The class defines metadata for arguments that can be provided to a prompt. These arguments are used to customize or parameterize prompts when they are retrieved using requests.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets a human-readable description of the argument's purpose and expected values.
member.remarks: This description helps developers understand what information should be provided for this argument and how it will affect the generated prompt.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument.Required
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this argument must be provided when requesting the prompt.
member.remarks: When set to , the client must include this argument when making a request. If a required argument is missing, the server should respond with an error.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptListChangedNotificationParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of prompts it offers has changed.

remarks.para: This notification can be issued by servers without any previous subscription from the client.
See the schema for details.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage
member.summary: Represents a message within the Model Context Protocol (MCP) system, used for communication between clients and AI models.

remarks.para: A encapsulates content sent to or received from AI models in the Model Context Protocol. Each message has a specific role ( or ) and contains content, which can be text, images, audio, or embedded resources.

This class is similar to , but with enhanced support for embedding resources from the MCP server. It serves as a core data structure in the MCP message exchange flow, particularly in prompt formation and model responses.
objects are typically used in collections within to represent complete conversations or prompt sequences. They can be converted to and from objects using the extension methods and .
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content
member.summary: Gets or sets the content of the message, which can be text, image, audio, or an embedded resource.
member.remarks: The object contains all the message payload, whether it's simple text, base64-encoded binary data (for images/audio), or a reference to an embedded resource. The property indicates the specific content type.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Role
member.summary: Gets or sets the role of the message sender, specifying whether it's from a "user" or an "assistant".
member.remarks: In the Model Context Protocol, each message must have a clear role assignment to maintain the conversation flow. User messages represent queries or inputs from users, while assistant messages represent responses generated by AI models.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptsCapability
member.summary: Represents the server's capability to provide predefined prompt templates that clients can use.

remarks.para: The prompts capability allows a server to expose a collection of predefined prompt templates that clients can discover and use. These prompts can be static (defined in the ) or dynamically generated through handlers.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptsCapability.ListChanged
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this server supports notifications for changes to the prompt list.
member.remarks: When set to , the server will send notifications using when prompts are added, removed, or modified. Clients can register handlers for these notifications to refresh their prompt cache. This capability enables clients to stay synchronized with server-side changes to available prompts.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams
member.summary: Represents the parameters used with a request from a client to get a resource provided by a server.

remarks.para: The server will respond with a containing the resulting resource data.

Alternatively, if the resource URI uses the https:// scheme, clients may fetch the resource directly from the web instead of using . Servers should only use the https:// scheme when the client is able to fetch and load the resource directly on its own.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams.Uri
member.summary: Gets or sets the URI of the resource to read. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.

member.remarks: See the schema for details.

member.summary: Gets or sets a list of objects that this resource contains.
member.remarks: This property contains the actual content of the requested resource, which can be either text-based () or binary (). The type of content included depends on the resource being accessed.

member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to request that the server invoke a specific tool.

member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to request a list of the server's prompts.

member.summary: The name of the request method sent by the client to get a prompt provided by the server.

member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to request a list of the server's resources.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to read a specific server resource.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesTemplatesList
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to request a list of the server's resource templates.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesSubscribe
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to request notifications from the server whenever a particular resource changes.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesUnsubscribe
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to request unsubscribing from notifications from the server.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.RootsList
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the server to request a list of the client's roots.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Ping
member.summary: The name of the request method sent by either endpoint to check that the connected endpoint is still alive.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to the server to adjust the logging level.
member.remarks: This request allows clients to control which log messages they receive from the server by setting a minimum severity threshold. After processing this request, the server will send log messages with severity at or above the specified level to the client as notifications.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to the server to ask for completion suggestions.
member.remarks: This is used to provide autocompletion-like functionality for arguments in a resource reference or a prompt template. The client provides a reference (resource or prompt), argument name, and partial value, and the server responds with matching completion options.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.SamplingCreateMessage
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the server to sample a large language model (LLM) via the client.
member.remarks: This request allows servers to utilize an LLM available on the client side to generate text or image responses based on provided messages. It is part of the sampling capability in the Model Context Protocol and enables servers to access client-side AI models without needing direct API access to those models.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ElicitationCreate
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the server to elicit additional information from the user via the client.

remarks.para: This request is used when the server needs more information from the client to proceed with a task or interaction. Servers can request structured data from users, with optional JSON schemas to validate responses (form mode), or request URL mode (out-of-band) user interaction via navigation for sensitive operations.

Two modes are supported:
form: In-band elicitation where structured data is collected and returned to the server
url: URL mode (out-of-band) elicitation for sensitive operations like OAuth or payments

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Initialize
member.summary: The name of the request method sent from the client to the server when it first connects, asking it to initialize.
member.remarks: The initialize request is the first request sent by the client to the server. It provides client information and capabilities to the server during connection establishment. The server responds with its own capabilities and information, establishing the protocol version and available features for the session.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksGet
member.summary: The name of the request method to retrieve task status.
member.remarks: Requestors poll for task completion by sending tasks/get requests. They should respect the pollInterval provided in responses when determining polling frequency.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksResult
member.summary: The name of the request method to retrieve the result of a completed task.
member.remarks: This request blocks until the task reaches a terminal status (completed, failed, or cancelled). The result structure matches the original request type (e.g., CallToolResult for tools/call).

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksList
member.summary: The name of the request method to retrieve a list of tasks with pagination support.

member.name: F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksCancel
member.summary: The name of the request method to explicitly cancel a task.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource

member.summary: Gets or sets the URI of this resource.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets a description of what this resource represents.

remarks.para: This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a \"hint\" to the model.
The description should provide clear context about the resource's content, format, and purpose. This helps AI models make better decisions about when to access or reference the resource.
Client applications can also use this description for display purposes in user interfaces or to help users understand the available resources.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.MimeType
member.summary: Gets or sets the MIME type of this resource.

remarks.para: specifies the format of the resource content, helping clients to properly interpret and display the data. Common MIME types include "text/plain" for plain text, "application/pdf" for PDF documents, "image/png" for PNG images, and "application/json" for JSON data.
This property can be if the MIME type is unknown or not applicable for the resource.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Annotations
member.summary: Gets or sets optional annotations for the resource.
member.remarks: These annotations can be used to specify the intended audience (, , or both) and the priority level of the resource. Clients can use this information to filter or prioritize resources for different roles.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Size
member.summary: Gets or sets the size of the raw resource content (before base64 encoding), in bytes, if known.
member.remarks: This can be used by applications to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Icons
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this resource.
member.remarks: This can be used by clients to display the resource's icon in a user interface.

member.remarks: Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate
member.summary: Represents a known resource template that the server is capable of reading.
member.remarks: Resource templates provide metadata about resources available on the server, including how to construct URIs for those resources.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.UriTemplate
member.summary: Gets or sets the URI template (according to RFC 6570) that can be used to construct resource URIs.

member.summary: Gets or sets a description of what this resource template represents.

remarks.para: This description helps clients understand the purpose and content of resources that can be generated from this template. It can be used by client applications to provide context about available resource types or to display in user interfaces.
For AI models, this description can serve as a hint about when and how to use the resource template, enhancing the model's ability to generate appropriate URIs.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.MimeType

remarks.para: Specifies the expected format of resources that can be generated from this template. This helps clients understand what type of content to expect when accessing resources created using this template.
Common MIME types include "text/plain" for plain text, "application/pdf" for PDF documents, "image/png" for PNG images, or "application/json" for JSON data.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.Annotations

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Root
member.summary: Represents a root URI and its metadata in the Model Context Protocol.
member.remarks: Root URIs serve as entry points for resource navigation, typically representing top-level directories or container resources that can be accessed and traversed. Roots provide a hierarchical structure for organizing and accessing resources within the protocol. Each root has a URI that uniquely identifies it and optional metadata like a human-readable name.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RootsCapability
member.summary: Represents a client capability that enables root resource discovery in the Model Context Protocol.

remarks.para: When present in , it indicates that the client supports listing root URIs that serve as entry points for resource navigation.

The roots capability establishes a mechanism for servers to discover and access the hierarchical structure of resources provided by a client. Root URIs represent top-level entry points from which servers can navigate to access specific resources.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RootsCapability.ListChanged
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the client supports notifications for changes to the roots list.
member.remarks: When set to , the client can notify servers when roots are added, removed, or modified, allowing servers to refresh their roots cache accordingly. This enables servers to stay synchronized with client-side changes to available roots.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability

remarks.para: This capability enables the MCP client to respond to sampling requests from an MCP server.
When this capability is enabled, an MCP server can request the client to generate content using an AI model. The client must set a to process these requests.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability.Context
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the client supports context inclusion via includeContext parameter.
member.remarks: If not declared, servers should only use includeContext: "none".

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability.Tools
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the client supports tool use via tools and toolChoice parameters.

member.summary: Represents the sampling context capability.

member.name: T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage
member.summary: Represents a message issued to or received from an LLM API within the Model Context Protocol.

remarks.para: A encapsulates content sent to or received from AI models in the Model Context Protocol. The message has a role ( or ) and content which can be text, images, audio, tool uses, or tool results.
objects are typically used in collections within to represent prompts or queries for LLM sampling. They form the core data structure for text generation requests within the Model Context Protocol.
If content contains any , then all content items must be . Tool results cannot be mixed with text, image, or audio content in the same message.
See the schema for details.

member.name: P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage.Content
member.summary: Gets or sets the content of the message.
